summary: Detect and fix methodology gaps identified through /retro project retrospectives
preconditions: at least one /retro has been completed with methodology findings
strategy: Read all meta/retro-*.md files to collect methodology gap signals
Classify gaps: workflow (SKILL.md), reference (references/*.md), or evaluation (/check)
For workflow gaps: update SKILL.md command definitions or add conditional logic
For reference gaps: add new sections to existing reference files or create new ones
For evaluation gaps: update scoring rubrics with sub-type-specific weights
Validate: re-run the test scenario that exposed the gap to confirm fix
validation: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const f=fs.readdirSync('thriller-writing/references'); console.log('refs:', f.length); if(f.length<10) throw new Error('missing refs')"

summary: Integrate findings from external methodology benchmarks into the skill system
strategy: Read meta/benchmark-*.md to identify high-priority integration items
For each item: determine target file (existing reference or new file)
Apply minimum viable change: add new section, not rewrite existing content
Cross-reference with existing content to avoid duplication
Update README.md methodology sources table if new sources added
Update meta/CHANGELOG.md and meta/VERSION.md
validation: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const c=fs.readFileSync('meta/CHANGELOG.md','utf8'); if(!c.includes('v2')) throw new Error('changelog not updated')"

summary: Create novel thriller writing techniques by synthesizing multiple methodology sources
preconditions: system is stable (no active repair signals)
at least one test project completed
strategy: Identify the capability gap from test results or user feedback
Search across all reference files for related partial solutions
Synthesize a new technique that combines insights from 2+ sources
Write the technique as a new section in the most relevant reference file
If the technique is substantial enough, create a new reference file
Test by applying the technique to the active project (mirror-visitor)
Record the innovation in meta/CHANGELOG.md

summary: Reverse-engineer published thriller works to extract techniques not in current methodology
strategy: Read the autopsy report for techniques marked as 'method cannot explain'
For each unexplainable technique: formalize it into a reusable pattern
Determine which reference file should contain the new pattern
Add with [Experiment] tag for future validation
Cross-reference: does this technique conflict with any existing principle?

summary: Generalize visual novel content into an engine-neutral package with stable node taxonomy and import-friendly exchange format
preconditions: interactive-fiction project uses manifest plus nodes directory layout
strategy: Refine the node taxonomy around start scene ending and explicit logic nodes
Keep prose presentation and logic separated in the compiled contract
Preserve declarative conditions and effects instead of engine-specific scripting
Validate the result as a single compiled exchange artifact

summary: Strengthen the local authoring loop by compiling markdown-plus-yaml projects and running deterministic graph doctor checks
preconditions: authoring layout uses one node file per runtime node
strategy: Compile project folders into one story.json artifact
Track the source file for each compiled node
Detect missing targets and unreachable runtime nodes
Keep the workflow local and deterministic before any importer step

summary: Scale the one-node-one-file visual novel layout with chapter route and tag metadata plus recursive node discovery
preconditions: the package already has a stable compiled contract
strategy: Allow nested folders under nodes
Add chapter route and tags to node metadata
Keep source files small and semantically scoped
Preserve the same compiled exchange contract regardless of folder depth

summary: Model interactive thriller quality as reader pull, deductive integrity, and playable consequence across explicit capability planes
preconditions: the project already uses a file-first visual novel package layout
strategy: Define quality planes instead of relying on vague taste
Name failure modes for suspense, logic, convergence, and endings
Turn narrative taste into acceptance checks that a later round can challenge

summary: Run a file-only 100-round thriller evolution loop that scaffolds rounds, checkpoints, and local logs without requiring a database
preconditions: the repository can run local node scripts
strategy: Expand a 10-band plan into 100 concrete rounds
Generate round briefs, checkpoints, and run state under meta/runs
Keep generated run artifacts local and git-ignored while preserving the method in code

summary: Treat investigation clues as a modular file-level ledger and audit route fairness before reveal or ending import
preconditions: the package supports node-level thriller metadata
strategy: Keep clue definitions in a dedicated systems file
Track which clues support each reveal or ending
Audit required clues against all reachable route states
Record fairness rules as reusable local references

summary: Build and refine the shot sequence logic system: cut reasons, information DAG, film idiom FSMs, cognitive continuity rules
preconditions: storyboard/SKILL.md exists with basic shot taxonomy
strategy: Identify logical gaps in shot-to-shot reasoning
Formalize cut justification rules (information/reaction/emphasis)
Build information dependency DAG model for shot sequences
Encode film idioms as finite state machines with decision rules
Integrate cognitive neuroscience findings (Magliano, Liao) as validation constraints
Create error dictionary for common logic violations
validation: storyboard/references/shot-logic.md exists and contains REQUIRES/PROVIDES/RAISES model

summary: Create and refine LLM-specific guidance for storyboard decomposition based on academic research on LLM screenplay generation
strategy: Analyze LLM weaknesses from R², DSR, and survey papers
Define forced chain-of-thought protocols for each decomposition step
Create self-audit protocol with computable quality metrics
Implement decompose-then-generate strategy with intermediate representations
Define LLM-specific traps and prevention measures
validation: storyboard/references/llm-guidance.md exists and contains self-audit protocol

summary: Build storyboard quality evaluation system with scoring rubrics and director autopsy framework
preconditions: storyboard decomposition algorithm with logic verification exists
strategy: Define multi-dimension scoring rubric for storyboard quality
Build /autopsy-visual command for reverse-engineering film shot choices
Create weighted evaluation that adapts to genre (thriller vs action vs drama)
Integrate computable metrics with human-judgment dimensions

summary: Strengthen integration bridges between storyboard module and thriller-writing, interactive-fiction, and Story Platform MCP
preconditions: storyboard module is stable with logic verification
strategy: Map storyboard outputs to VN node directives (character positions, backgrounds, audio)
Map storyboard outputs to MCP tool calls (node creation, asset requests)
Create conversion protocols between storyboard JSON and VN package format
Define how clue-design from thriller-writing maps to fair-play shot design

summary: Run a file-only 100-round storyboard evolution loop across 10 thematic bands
preconditions: the repository can run local node scripts
strategy: Expand a 10-band plan into 100 concrete rounds
Generate round briefs, checkpoints, and run state under meta/runs
Keep generated run artifacts local and git-ignored
